description: A point in respect to which two or more things are dissimilar. In Phi, this is a neutral term, simply noting a distinction without implying conflict or hierarchy.
rationale: Composed from the established roots 'ke' (a spark, a point) and 'no' (mind, thought). The word's structure literally means 'a point of thought.' This defines difference in a neutral, cognitive way—as a distinct idea or a specific point of view, aligning with Peace Linguistics by avoiding oppositional framing.
